napoleon-bonaparte.keyAchievements.6.des: In 1801, he signed the Concordat with Pope Pius VII, seeking reconciliation with the Roman Catholic Church and recognizing Protestantism.
napoleon-bonaparte.keyAchievements.7.des: After becoming emperor, he pursued modernization policies to make Paris, the capital of France, the greatest capital in the world.
napoleon-bonaparte.faq.0.question: Was Napoleon actually short in height?
napoleon-bonaparte.faq.0.answer: No, his actual height was 167-168 cm, which was taller than the average height of 164 cm for French soldiers in their 20s at the time. The misconception that he was short arose from differences in systems of measurement and confusion with Napoleon III.
napoleon-bonaparte.faq.1.question: How did Napoleon die?
napoleon-bonaparte.faq.1.answer: The official cause of death is known to be stomach cancer, but theories of poisoning have also been raised due to high levels of arsenic found in his hair.
napoleon-bonaparte.faq.2.question: Was Beethoven's Symphony 'Eroica' dedicated to Napoleon?
napoleon-bonaparte.faq.2.answer: Beethoven considered Napoleon a hero of republicanism and named his Symphony No. 3 'Bonaparte'. However, when Napoleon became emperor, Beethoven was disappointed, withdrew the dedication, and changed the title to 'Eroica'.
napoleon-bonaparte.faq.3.question: What marked the beginning of Napoleon's downfall?
napoleon-bonaparte.faq.3.answer: His downfall began with the shortage of materials and daily necessities caused by the Continental System in 1806, the failure to occupy the Iberian Peninsula, and the disastrous Russian campaign in 1812.
king-sejong.timeline.0.event: Born in Junsu-bang, Hanseong, as the third son of King Taejong and Queen Wongyeong.
king-sejong.timeline.1.event: Invested as the new Crown Prince after his older brother, Grand Prince Yangnyeong, was deposed.
king-sejong.timeline.2.event: Ascended the throne as the 4th king of Joseon at Geunjeongjeon in Gyeongbokgung Palace following King Taejong's abdication.
king-sejong.timeline.3.event: Appointed Yi Jong-mu as the Commander-in-Chief of the Three Provinces, leading an expedition of 227 warships and 17,000 soldiers to conquer Tsushima Island.
king-sejong.timeline.4.event: Established the Hall of Worthies (Jiphyeonjeon) within the palace as a policy research institute to operate the centralized ruling system.
king-sejong.timeline.5.event: Secured exemption from the tribute of virgins and gold/silver to the Ming dynasty.
king-sejong.timeline.6.event: A military force led by Kim Jong-seo swept away the Jurchen tribes in the Hamgil province region and established the Six Garrisons, pioneering the northern territory.
king-sejong.timeline.7.event: Lamenting that commoners could not easily learn Chinese characters, the writing system of the time, he personally created 28 new letters.
king-sejong.timeline.8.event: Promulgated the Hunminjeongeum, which he had personally created.
king-sejong.timeline.9.event: His illness worsened, and he passed away after moving his residence to the home of Grand Prince Yeongeung.
king-sejong.keyAchievements.0.title: Creation of Hunminjeongeum
king-sejong.keyAchievements.0.descriptio: Created Hunminjeongeum, an efficient and scientific writing system that commoners could easily learn.
king-sejong.keyAchievements.1.title: Support for Scientific and Technological Advancement
king-sejong.keyAchievements.1.descriptio: Provided full support for the invention of the armillary sphere (Honcheonui), sundial (Angbuilgu), water clock (Jagyeongnu), and rain gauge (Cheugugi), and sponsored scholars like Jang Yeong-sil and Choe Hae-san.
king-sejong.keyAchievements.2.title: Strengthening National Defense
king-sejong.keyAchievements.2.descriptio: Dispatched Yi Jong-mu to conquer Tsushima Island, and sent figures like Choe Yun-deok and Kim Jong-seo to the north to pioneer the Four Counties and Six Garrisons, expanding the national borders.
king-sejong.keyAchievements.3.title: Establishment of the Political System
king-sejong.keyAchievements.3.descriptio: Appointed officials like Hwang Hui, Maeng Sa-seong, Yun Hoe, and Kim Jong-seo to oversee state affairs, which became the origin of the State Council system (Uijeongbu seosaje).
king-sejong.keyAchievements.4.title: Establishment of the Taxation System
king-sejong.keyAchievements.4.descriptio: Established Joseon's land tax system by enacting tribute laws such as the six-grade land system (Jeonbun 6-deungbeop) and the nine-grade harvest system (Yeonbun 9-deungbeop).
king-sejong.keyAchievements.5.title: Compilation of Books
king-sejong.keyAchievements.5.descriptio: Compiled books in various fields, including 'Songs of the Moon Shining on a Thousand Rivers' (Worin cheongang jigok), 'Songs of the Dragons Flying to Heaven' (Yongbi eocheonga), 'Straight Talk on Farming' (Nongsa jikseol), and 'History of Goryeo' (Goryeosa).
king-sejong.faq.0.question: When was King Sejong the Great born?
king-sejong.faq.0.answer: King Sejong was born on May 15, 1397 (April 10 on the lunar calendar) in Junsu-bang, Hanseong, as the third son of King Taejong and Queen Wongyeong.
king-sejong.faq.1.question: How did King Sejong the Great ascend to the throne?
king-sejong.faq.1.answer: He was invested as the Crown Prince when his older brother, Grand Prince Yangnyeong, was deposed, and ascended the throne in 1418 following King Taejong's abdication.
king-sejong.faq.2.question: What is King Sejong the Great's greatest achievement?
king-sejong.faq.2.answer: One of his greatest achievements is the creation of Hunminjeongeum, an efficient and scientific writing system that commoners could easily learn.
king-sejong.faq.3.question: What achievements did King Sejong the Great leave in the field of national defense?
king-sejong.faq.3.answer: He dispatched Yi Jong-mu to conquer Tsushima Island and sent figures like Choe Yun-deok and Kim Jong-seo to the north to pioneer the Four Counties and Six Garrisons, expanding the national borders.
king-sejong.faq.4.question: When did King Sejong the Great pass away?
king-sejong.faq.4.answer: King Sejong passed away on March 30, 1450 (February 17 on the lunar calendar).
genghis-khan.timeline.0.event: Temüjin was born to his father Yesügei and mother Hoelun in Delüün Boldog near the Onon River.
genghis-khan.timeline.1.event: Known as the birth year of Genghis Khan, although historical records vary (the current Mongolian memorial day is November 14).
genghis-khan.timeline.2.event: Visited Dei Sechen of the Khongirad tribe and formally married his daughter, Börte.
genghis-khan.timeline.3.event: Elected as the tribal chief of the ancient Khamag Mongol confederation.
genghis-khan.timeline.4.event: Various lords of the Borjigin clan acclaimed Temüjin as the Khan of Khamag Mongol, granting him the title of Genghis Khan.
genghis-khan.timeline.5.event: The power of the Khamag Mongol tribe grew, leading to the Battle of the Thirteen Sides against Jamukha at the Dalan Balzhut plain.
genghis-khan.timeline.6.event: Toghrul Khan, who had been ousted due to internal conflict within the Keraites, was restored to power with Temüjin's support.
genghis-khan.timeline.7.event: With the help of the Keraites, Temüjin attacked and defeated the Jurkin clan, unifying the Borjigin clan.
genghis-khan.timeline.8.event: Launched an expedition and subjugated the remaining Merkit tribe in the northern part of the plateau.
genghis-khan.timeline.9.event: Held a Kurultai at Temeen Keer and declared an expedition to conquer the Naiman tribe.
genghis-khan.timeline.10.event: Genghis Khan allied with the Keraites to attack the Naiman tribe in the Altai Mountains region.
genghis-khan.timeline.11.event: The remaining forces of the Tatar tribe, along with the Merkits and Tayichiuds, formed an alliance and acclaimed Jamukha as Gur Khan. Annihilated Jamukha's alliance at the Battle of Koyiten against the northeastern coalition.
genghis-khan.timeline.12.event: Temüjin engaged in battle with the Tatar tribe, killed their chieftain Jalin-bukha, and executed over a hundred remaining Tatar men.
genghis-khan.timeline.13.event: Ong Khan ambushed Temüjin's pastureland due to a plot by Senggum, and Temüjin retaliated with a surprise attack on the main Keraite force, achieving a major victory.
genghis-khan.timeline.14.event: The Khamag Mongol army drove out and conquered the Naiman tribe.
genghis-khan.timeline.15.event: Temüjin defeated the remaining major powers on the Mongolian plateau, the Western Naimans and Northern Merkits, and executed Jamukha.
genghis-khan.timeline.16.event: At a tribal council held on the banks of the Onon River, Temüjin was acclaimed as Genghis Khan and founded the Mongol Empire.
genghis-khan.timeline.17.event: Sent his son Jochi to conquer and subjugate the forest tribes. Emperor Xiangzong of Western Xia promised subjugation to the Mongols and sent his daughter to marry Genghis Khan.
genghis-khan.timeline.18.event: Concluded a peace treaty with Western Xia, securing recognition of Mongol suzerainty. Subjugated the Uyghur Kingdom of the Tian Shan.
genghis-khan.timeline.19.event: Sent Kublai to conquer the Karluks, a Turkic nomadic tribe; their chief, Arslan Khan, surrendered. Genghis Khan notified the Jin dynasty that he would cease tributary relations.
genghis-khan.timeline.20.event: Declared war on the Jin dynasty and commenced an invasion, defeating a Jin army of 400,000 at the Battle of Yehuling.
genghis-khan.timeline.21.event: Led an army to attack the Jin dynasty, conquering and annexing Jin territory south of the Great Wall.
genghis-khan.timeline.22.event: When the Jin dynasty sued for peace, he took Princess Qiguo as his wife, received an indemnity, and retreated.
genghis-khan.timeline.23.event: The Mongol army besieged and captured Zhongdu (modern Beijing), the former capital of the Jin dynasty.
genghis-khan.timeline.24.event: Genghis Khan sent a trade delegation to the Khwarazmian Empire, but Inalchuq, the governor of Otrar, killed the envoys.
genghis-khan.timeline.25.event: Genghis Khan launched an invasion of Khwarazmia with an army of 200,000 men.
genghis-khan.timeline.26.event: Genghis Khan withdrew after concluding his campaign of vengeance against the Khwarazmian Empire.
genghis-khan.timeline.27.event: Hearing news of a rebellion in Western Xia, Genghis Khan departed from Peshawar and returned to the Mongolian plateau.
genghis-khan.timeline.28.event: Genghis Khan arrived on the Mongolian plateau.
genghis-khan.timeline.29.event: The Mongol army invaded Western Xia, attacking its border regions.
genghis-khan.timeline.30.event: Genghis Khan stationed his main camp at Liupan Mountain, accepted the surrender of Western Xia, but rejected a peace proposal from the Jin dynasty.
genghis-khan.keyAchievements.0.title: Founding of the Mongol Empire and the First Khagan
genghis-khan.keyAchievements.0.descripti: At the Kurultai in 1206, he was acclaimed as Genghis Khan, founded the Mongol Empire, and became its first Khagan.
genghis-khan.keyAchievements.1.title: Unification of the Mongol Tribes
genghis-khan.keyAchievements.1.descripti: As a military and political leader who united various Mongol tribes, he conquered the tribes of the Mongolian steppes.
genghis-khan.keyAchievements.2.title: Conquest of Vast Territories
genghis-khan.keyAchievements.2.descripti: He conquered a vast expanse of territory, ranging from the Korean peninsula to Central Asia and Eastern Europe, including Mongolia and regions of China.
genghis-khan.keyAchievements.3.title: Organization of a Merit-Based Army
genghis-khan.keyAchievements.3.descripti: He is regarded as one of history's greatest conquerors and most successful military leaders by commanding a strong army based on meritocracy.
genghis-khan.keyAchievements.4.title: Establishment of the Mongolian Script
genghis-khan.keyAchievements.4.descripti: With the help of a retainer from the Naiman tribe, he assisted in establishing a written script for the Mongolian language.
genghis-khan.faq.0.question: What was Genghis Khan's real name?
genghis-khan.faq.0.answer: Genghis Khan's real name was Borjigin Temüjin.
genghis-khan.faq.1.question: When did Genghis Khan ascend to the throne of the Mongol Empire?
genghis-khan.faq.1.answer: At a tribal council held on the banks of the Onon River in February 1206, Temüjin was acclaimed as Genghis Khan, founded the Mongol Empire, and ascended as its first Khagan.
genghis-khan.faq.2.question: How is Genghis Khan evaluated?
genghis-khan.faq.2.answer: In Mongolia, he is revered as a god or a national founding hero, but in regions of Eastern Europe and Western Asia that were invaded, there are conflicting evaluations of him as a mass murderer, looter, and destroyer of civilizations.
genghis-khan.faq.3.question: How did Genghis Khan's father die?
genghis-khan.faq.3.answer: When Genghis Khan was 9 years old, his father Yesügei was poisoned by the Tatar tribe, who had a long-standing feud with the Mongol Borjigin clan.
genghis-khan.faq.4.question: Why did Genghis Khan attack the Khwarazmian Empire?
genghis-khan.faq.4.answer: Because Inalchuq, the governor of Otrar, massacred an Islamic trade caravan of 450 people under Genghis Khan's protection, and the Shah of the Khwarazmian Empire refused to provide compensation for this.
alexander-the-great.timeline.0.event: Born in Pella, the capital of Macedonia, as the son of Philip II and Olympias.
alexander-the-great.timeline.1.event: Alongside Philip II, defeated the allied forces of Athens and Thebes at the Battle of Chaeronea.
alexander-the-great.timeline.2.event: Fled Macedonia with his mother but returned six months later.
alexander-the-great.timeline.3.event: Succeeded to the Macedonian throne at the age of 20 following the assassination of his father, Philip II.
alexander-the-great.timeline.4.event: To stabilize the northern borders, he launched a campaign to suppress rebellions, defeating Thrace, the Triballi, the Getae tribes, and Illyria, and subsequently suppressed the Theban revolt, destroying the city.
alexander-the-great.timeline.5.event: Crossed the Hellespont to begin the invasion of the Achaemenid Empire (First Persian Empire) and won the Battle of the Granicus.
